Phase-31.NAME: User Cabinet MTProto UX
Phase-31.Goal: Expose the free indefinite Telegram MTProto proxy in the compact KrotPN user cabinet.

NonGoals.item-1: No domain edge cutover in this phase.
NonGoals.item-2: No admin MTProto controls in this phase.

Steps.step-1: Add authenticated /api/v1/mtproto/proxy endpoint returning the current user's safe MTProto response payload.
Steps.step-2: Add frontend API types and query hooks for MTProto proxy state.
Steps.step-3: Add compact dashboard card with open Telegram, copy link, copy server, copy port, and copy secret actions.
Steps.step-4: Add unavailable, pending, degraded, and reissue-required states with concise mobile copy.
Steps.step-5: Verify mobile viewports and frontend build while preserving VPN trial and config actions.

Verification.command: cd backend && PYTHONPATH=. ../.venv/bin/python -m pytest -o addopts='' tests/test_mtproto_api.py -v
Phase-31 mobile smoke for dashboard at 360x740, 390x844, and 430x932

AcceptanceCriteria.item-1: Verified users can view and copy Telegram proxy details from the main dashboard.
AcceptanceCriteria.item-2: Unverified or degraded users receive safe, actionable states.
AcceptanceCriteria.item-3: The card does not introduce horizontal scroll or hide primary VPN subscription/config actions.
AcceptanceCriteria.item-4: Only the authenticated owner receives full link and secret fields.

Risks.risk-1: Showing too much proxy data in first viewport can clutter the mobile cabinet.
Risks.risk-2: Clipboard behavior can fail on some mobile browsers; manual copy fields must remain usable.
